questions.question: What is the main purpose of the word count program in Hadoop and Spark?
options: To count words in a text
questions.explanation: The word count program is used to count the number of occurrences of each word in a given text.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a key component of the word count program in Spark?
questions.explanation: HDFS is a distributed file system, not a key component of the word count program.

questions.question: What happens to the output of the word count program in Hadoop?
options: It is processed by the map-reduce framework
It is sent to the user
questions.explanation: The output of the word count program is stored in HDFS, where it can be processed further or retrieved by the user.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using Spark for the word count program?
questions.explanation: Spark offers faster processing times due to its distributed architecture and in-memory processing capabilities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common use case for the word count program?
questions.explanation: Analyzing customer reviews is a common use case for the word count program, as it can help identify popular keywords and phrases.
